message.reasoning_content:
Let me carefully parse this crochet pattern for an Airpods Case.

**Project Title**: "Airpods Case" (as hinted)

**Materials**:
- DK yarn in the colour(s) of your choice
- A 3mm crochet hook
- A stitch marker
- A darning needle
- A button if you aren't going to crochet one

**Abbreviations** from the Key section:
- CH: Chain
- HTC: Half Treble Crochet
- DC: Double Crochet
- FO: Fasten Off

**Parts**:

The pattern has:
1. Body (worked in rounds)
2. Flap (worked in rows, but seems to be part of the Body? Let me re-read)

Actually, looking at the structure:
- The pattern starts with "Body:" and includes Round 1, Rounds 2-6, Round 7
- Then "Now we will begin to work the flap" - this has Row 1, Rows 2-9, Row 10, Row 11
- Then "Button:" section

So parts could be: Body, Flap, Button. But the flap seems to be a continuation of the body. Let me think about how to split this.

Looking at the pattern structure:
- Body section contains rounds (Round 1, Rounds 2-6, Round 7)
- Flap section contains rows (Row 1, Rows 2-9, Row 10, Row 11)
- Button section

I'll structure as three parts: "Body", "Flap", "Button".

Now let me parse each round/row carefully:

**Body Part:**

Round 1: "2 HTC in first stitch, 1 HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2HTC in the next stitch, working in the other side of the chain, 2 HTC in the next stitch, 1HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2 HTC in the last stitch, join to the 1st HTC. You should have 24 stitches in total."

Instructions say "Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker." - this is a non-stitch instruction that comes after Round 1. I'll include it in the rawInstruction for Round 1 since it's part of the same round's guidance.

Actually wait, let me re-read. Round 1 ends with "...join to the 1st HTC. You should have 24 stitches in total. Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker."

The "Mark the beginning..." is an additional instruction. I think I should keep it in the rawInstruction for Round 1 as it's part of the same paragraph. The targetStitchCount is 24 (explicitly stated).

Rounds 2-6: "1 HTC in each stitch all the way around (24)" - This is a range expansion. I'll create one sentinel with rangeStartNumber=2, rangeEndNumber=6, rawInstruction="1 HTC in each stitch all the way around", targetStitchCount=24.

Round 7: "1 DC in each stitch all the way around (24)" - targetStitchCount=24.

**Flap Part:**

Now there's text "Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth." - This is a transition instruction but not really a separate round. I could include it as a context note or skip it since it's not a stitch instruction. Actually, I'll treat it as a non-stitch instruction round.

Row 1: "1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn. (10)" - targetStitchCount=10.

Rows 2-9: "1 HTC in each stitch all the way across, CH 2, turn (10)" - range expansion, rangeStartNumber=2, rangeEndNumber=9, rawInstruction="1 HTC in each stitch all the way across, CH 2, turn", targetStitchCount=10.

Row 10: "2HTCtog, 1HTC, 1HTC, CH2, skip the next 2 stitches, 1HTC, 1HTC, 2HTCtog, CH2, turn (8)" - targetStitchCount=8.

Row 11: "1HTC in each stitch across, FO. (8)" - targetStitchCount=8.

**Button Part:**

"Start with a magic ring" - could be a non-stitch instruction or part of the button setup.

Round 1: "work 12 treble crochet, join. (12)" - targetStitchCount=12.

Round 2: "SS into each stitch around (12)" - targetStitchCount=12.

Then: "Line the button up with where the button hole on the flap lies when in position at the front of the pouch, (you can mark this with a stitch marker) and sew into place." - This is a non-stitch instruction.

"Weave in the ends." - non-stitch instruction.

"And you're done!" - not really actionable.

Now, about the Flap part - should it be a separate part or a continuation of Body? The pattern says "Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth." This suggests the flap is distinct. I'll make it a separate part.

For the transition text "Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth." - I'll include it as a non-stitch instruction round at the start of the Flap part. Actually, it's more of a heading/transition. Let me think... I'll include it as a non-stitch round with title "Begin flap" or similar.

Wait, the pattern text for Row 1 includes "1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn. (10)". But looking more carefully... "1DC, 1DC" - that's two DC stitches? Or is it a typo? Let me keep it as written.

Now for the Body section, there's also the initial "CH 12" and "Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain, work as follows,". This is setup for Round 1. I should include it as part of Round 1 or as a separate non-stitch round.

Let me include "CH 12" as part of the Round 1 raw instruction or as a separate instruction. Actually, looking at the pattern structure:
- "CH 12" 
- "Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain, work as follows,"
- Round 1: ...

I think "CH 12" is the foundation chain. I'll make it a separate non-stitch round before Round 1, or include it in Round 1. Let me make it a separate round with title "Foundation Chain" or "CH 12". But actually, it's not a round - it's the foundation chain. Let me just include it as part of Round 1's raw instruction or as a separate instruction round.

Actually, I'll treat "CH 12" and the setup instruction as a pre-round instruction. Let me make it a non-stitch round: "CH 12" with rawInstruction "CH 12" and summary about foundation chain. Then Round 1 starts.

Hmm, but Round 1 says "2 HTC in first stitch, 1 HTC in each of the next 8 stitches..." - the "first stitch" refers to the 2nd chain from hook. So the CH 12 is integral. I'll keep it as a separate round "Foundation Chain" with title "Foundation Chain" and rawInstruction "CH 12. Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain."

Actually, let me just include the CH 12 and the "Starting..." text in the rawInstruction for a non-stitch round before Round 1.

Let me finalize my structure:

**Part 1: Body**
- Non-stitch: "Foundation Chain" / "CH 12" 
- Round 1: rawInstruction = "2 HTC in first stitch, 1 HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2HTC in the next stitch, working in the other side of the chain, 2 HTC in the next stitch, 1HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2 HTC in the last stitch, join to the 1st HTC. Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker.", targetStitchCount=24
- Rounds 2-6 (sentinel): rawInstruction = "1 HTC in each stitch all the way around", targetStitchCount=24, rangeStartNumber=2, rangeEndNumber=6
- Round 7: rawInstruction = "1 DC in each stitch all the way around", targetStitchCount=24

**Part 2: Flap**
- Non-stitch: "Begin flap" - rawInstruction = "Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth."
- Row 1: rawInstruction = "1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn.", targetStitchCount=10
- Rows 2-9 (sentinel): rawInstruction = "1 HTC in each stitch all the way across, CH 2, turn", targetStitchCount=10, rangeStartNumber=2, rangeEndNumber=9
- Non-stitch: "Button hole" - rawInstruction = "Now we will work our button hole." (this is a transition)
- Row 10: rawInstruction = "2HTCtog, 1HTC, 1HTC, CH2, skip the next 2 stitches, 1HTC, 1HTC, 2HTCtog, CH2, turn", targetStitchCount=8
- Row 11: rawInstruction = "1HTC in each stitch across, FO.", targetStitchCount=8

**Part 3: Button**
- Non-stitch: "Start button" - rawInstruction = "Start with a magic ring"
- Round 1: rawInstruction = "work 12 treble crochet, join.", targetStitchCount=12
- Round 2: rawInstruction = "SS into each stitch around", targetStitchCount=12
- Non-stitch: "Sew button" - rawInstruction = "Line the button up with where the button hole on the flap lies when in position at the front of the pouch, (you can mark this with a stitch marker) and sew into place."
- Non-stitch: "Weave in ends" - rawInstruction = "Weave in the ends."

Now, about the "And you're done!" - I'll skip this as it's not an actionable instruction.

Let me also consider the "Notes:" section - "The main body of the case is worked in rounds, once you reach the flap section you will work in rows." and "Special Skills: Magic ring." - These aren't really abbreviations or actionable instructions. I'll skip them.

Now for abbreviations:
- CH: Chain
- HTC: Half Treble Crochet
- DC: Double Crochet
- FO: Fasten Off

What about "SS" - slip stitch? It appears in the Button Round 2 but isn't defined in the key. Same with "2HTCtog" - 2 half treble crochet together. These aren't explicitly defined in the key section, so I won't include them unless they appear in the abbreviations section.

Wait, the pattern also uses "SS" (slip stitch) and "2HTCtog" but these aren't in the key. Since the pattern's key only lists CH, HTC, DC, FO, I'll only include those four.

Now for confidence: The pattern is fairly clear and well-structured. I'd say 0.93.

Let me also double-check: is there a "treble crochet" abbreviation? In the button section, it says "work 12 treble crochet" - but "treble crochet" isn't in the key. It's written out fully. So no abbreviation for it.

Also, "SS" appears but isn't in the key. I'll leave it out of abbreviations.

Now, for the "Special Skills: Magic ring." - This is listed under notes but isn't really an abbreviation. I'll skip it.

Let me now construct the JSON.

For the Body part, I need to decide: should the "CH 12" and setup be a separate round or part of Round 1? I think making it a separate non-stitch round makes sense:

Title: "Foundation Chain"
rawInstruction: "CH 12. Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain, work as follows."
summary: "Create a foundation chain of 12 and prepare to work into both sides."

Then Round 1 follows.

For the "Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker" - I'll include this in Round 1's rawInstruction.

Actually wait, let me re-read the original pattern more carefully:

"Body:
CH 12
Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one
side of the chain, work as follows,
Round 1: 2 HTC in first stitch, 1 HTC in each of the next 8
stitches, 2HTC in the next stitch, working in the other side of
the chain, 2 HTC in the next stitch, 1HTC in each of the next 8
stitches, 2 HTC in the last stitch, join to the 1st HTC. You
should have 24 stitches in total. Mark the beginning of your
round with a stitch marker."

The "CH 12" is before Round 1. I think I'll make it a separate non-stitch round.

OK, I think I have everything. Let me finalize.

For the Flap part, the transition "Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth." - I'll make this a non-stitch round with title "Begin Flap" or similar. Actually, the title should reflect the instruction. Let me use "Begin flap section" as title and the raw instruction as the original text.

For "Now we will work our button hole." - this is also a transition. I'll make it a non-stitch round with title "Button hole" and rawInstruction as the original text.

For the Button part:
- "Start with a magic ring" - non-stitch round with title "Magic ring" or similar
- Round 1: "work 12 treble crochet, join. (12)"
- Round 2: "SS into each stitch around (12)"
- "Line the button up..." - non-stitch round
- "Weave in the ends." - non-stitch round

Let me now construct the final JSON.

Actually, I need to think about the "Flap" part title more carefully. The pattern says "the flap" and then gives Row 1-11. Should I call the part "Flap"? Yes.

And for the Button part, the pattern has "Button:" as a heading. So part name "Button".

Let me finalize everything now.

One more thing: for Row 1 of the Flap, the raw instruction is: "1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn. (10)". The "(10)" indicates targetStitchCount of 10.

Wait, "1DC, 1DC" - that's two double crochets. Seems odd but I'll keep as written.

Now let me also think about summary strings:

- Round 1 (Body): "Work increases and single HTC stitches around both sides of the foundation chain to create an oval base, joining at the end."
- Rounds 2-6: "Work one half treble crochet in each stitch for five rounds."
- Round 7: "Work one double crochet in each stitch around."
- Row 1 (Flap): "Work two double crochets, chain 2, turn, then work 10 half treble crochets, chain 2, turn."
- Rows 2-9: "Work one half treble crochet in each stitch across for eight rows, chaining 2 and turning at the end of each."
- Row 10: "Work decreases and chain spaces to create a button hole."
- Row 11: "Work one half treble crochet in each stitch across and fasten off."
- Button Round 1: "Work 12 treble crochets into a magic ring and join."
- Button Round 2: "Slip stitch into each stitch around."

OK let me build the JSON now.

I need to be careful about the "targetStitchCount" for the range sentinel Rounds 2-6. The pattern says "(24)" so targetStitchCount is 24.

For the summary of Rounds 2-6: "Work one half treble crochet in each stitch for five rounds."

And for Rows 2-9: "Work one half treble crochet in each stitch across for eight rows, chaining 2 and turning at the end of each."

Now, for "CH 12" - should I include the "Starting in the 2nd chain..." text in the same round? The pattern says:

"CH 12
Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one
side of the chain, work as follows,
Round 1: ..."

I think I'll combine CH 12 and the setup text into one non-stitch instruction round.

Let me finalize:

Part 1: Body
- Round: "Foundation Chain", rawInstruction: "CH 12. Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain, work as follows.", summary: "Chain 12 to create the foundation, then prepare to work into one side of the chain starting from the second chain from the hook."
- Round 1, rawInstruction: "2 HTC in first stitch, 1 HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2HTC in the next stitch, working in the other side of the chain, 2 HTC in the next stitch, 1HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2 HTC in the last stitch, join to the 1st HTC. Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker.", targetStitchCount: 24
- Rounds 2-6 (sentinel), rawInstruction: "1 HTC in each stitch all the way around", targetStitchCount: 24, rangeStartNumber: 2, rangeEndNumber: 6
- Round 7, rawInstruction: "1 DC in each stitch all the way around", targetStitchCount: 24

Part 2: Flap
- Non-stitch: "Begin flap", rawInstruction: "Now we will begin to work the flap, from here on we will be working in rows back and forth."
- Row 1, rawInstruction: "1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn.", targetStitchCount: 10
- Rows 2-9 (sentinel), rawInstruction: "1 HTC in each stitch all the way across, CH 2, turn", targetStitchCount: 10, rangeStartNumber: 2, rangeEndNumber: 9
- Non-stitch: "Button hole", rawInstruction: "Now we will work our button hole."
- Row 10, rawInstruction: "2HTCtog, 1HTC, 1HTC, CH2, skip the next 2 stitches, 1HTC, 1HTC, 2HTCtog, CH2, turn", targetStitchCount: 8
- Row 11, rawInstruction: "1HTC in each stitch across, FO.", targetStitchCount: 8

Part 3: Button
- Non-stitch: "Start button", rawInstruction: "Start with a magic ring"
- Round 1, rawInstruction: "work 12 treble crochet, join.", targetStitchCount: 12
- Round 2, rawInstruction: "SS into each stitch around", targetStitchCount: 12
- Non-stitch: "Attach button", rawInstruction: "Line the button up with where the button hole on the flap lies when in position at the front of the pouch, (you can mark this with a stitch marker) and sew into place."
- Non-stitch: "Weave in ends", rawInstruction: "Weave in the ends."

I think this is comprehensive. Let me now call the function.

One more thing - the confidence. The pattern is quite clear but has a few slightly unusual aspects (like "1DC, 1DC" in Row 1 of Flap, and using treble crochet without abbreviation). I'll set confidence to 0.92.

Let me also double-check: the pattern says "You should have 24 stitches in total." for Round 1, which is explicit. Good.

For the "Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker" - I included it in Round 1's rawInstruction since it's part of the same paragraph and directly follows the stitch count statement. That seems appropriate.

Now, I should also consider: is there a "***" separator between Body and Flap sections? Yes, there is. And between Round 7 and "Now we will begin..." there's also "***". So the sections are clearly separated. I'll treat them as separate parts.

Let me also re-read to see if I missed anything:

"Round 2-6: 1 HTC in each stitch all the way around (24)" - sentinel
"Round 7: 1 DC in each stitch all the way around (24)" - normal

Then "***"

"Now we will begin to work the flap..."

"Row 1: 1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn. (10)"
"Row 2-9: 1 HTC in each stitch all the way across, CH 2, turn (10)" - sentinel

"Now we will work our button hole."

"Row 10: 2HTCtog, 1HTC, 1HTC, CH2, skip the next 2 stitches, 1HTC, 1HTC, 2HTCtog, CH2, turn (8)"
"Row 11: 1HTC in each stitch across, FO. (8)"

"Button:"
"Start with a magic ring,"
"Round 1: work 12 treble crochet, join. (12)"
"Round 2: SS into each stitch around (12)"
"Line the button up..."
"Weave in the ends."
"And you're done!"

Everything looks covered. I'm ready to call the function.
